questions.q: In the Dashavatara, which incarnation of Vishnu is Parashurama counted as?
questions.explanation: Parashurama is traditionally counted as the sixth of the ten principal avatars (Dashavatara) of Vishnu.

questions.q: What is the signature weapon of Parashurama, from which his name is derived?
questions.explanation: His name combines 'Rama' with 'parashu' (axe), the weapon he is said to have received from Shiva.

questions.q: Who is the father of Parashurama in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: Parashurama is the son of the sage Jamadagni, one of the Saptarishi.

questions.q: Who is the mother of Parashurama according to the Puranas?
questions.explanation: Parashurama's mother is Renuka, the wife of the sage Jamadagni.

questions.q: Which deity is said to have gifted the axe (parashu) to Parashurama?
questions.explanation: According to the Puranas, Parashurama bore the axe gifted to him by Shiva.

questions.q: Parashurama is described as belonging to which dual social role?
questions.explanation: He is uniquely a brahmin by birth who takes up the path of the warrior, making him a brahmin-warrior.

questions.q: Parashurama is said to remain deathless, placing him among which group of beings?
questions.explanation: He is counted among the chiranjivi, the long-lived or immortal ones, and is said to remain immortal.

questions.q: Whose corrupt rule did Parashurama set out to rid the earth of, according to the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Parashurama rid the earth of corrupt kshatriya tyrants who had abused their power.

questions.q: What does the name 'Parashurama' literally describe?
questions.explanation: 'Parashu' means axe, so Parashurama means 'Rama with the axe.'

questions.q: Of which great god is Parashurama regarded as an avatar (descent)?
questions.explanation: Parashurama is one of the Dashavatara, the incarnations through which Vishnu descends to restore dharma.

questions.q: In the classical Dashavatara sequence, which avatar of Vishnu immediately precedes Parashurama?
questions.explanation: The sequence runs through Vamana before reaching Parashurama, the sixth descent.

questions.q: In the classical Dashavatara sequence, which avatar of Vishnu immediately follows Parashurama?
questions.explanation: Parashurama, the sixth avatar, is followed by Rama, the seventh, in the classical sequence.

questions.q: Parashurama's father Jamadagni is traditionally numbered among which group of seven sages?
questions.explanation: Jamadagni is commonly listed among the Saptarishi, the seven great seers.

questions.q: What act of his father against Renuka is Parashurama famously remembered for carrying out in obedience?
options: Beheading his own mother at Jamadagni's command
questions.explanation: In the Puranic tale, Parashurama obeyed his father's command to behead Renuka and was later granted her restoration as a boon.

questions.q: What chief motive do the Puranas give for Parashurama's campaign against the kshatriyas?
questions.explanation: According to the Puranas, Parashurama avenged the death of his father Jamadagni, slain by kshatriyas.

questions.q: Which thousand-armed king is described in the Mahabharata and Puranas as the slayer of Jamadagni, provoking Parashurama's wrath?
questions.explanation: The Haihaya king Kartavirya Arjuna (Sahasrarjuna) and his sons are said to have killed Jamadagni, igniting Parashurama's vengeance.

questions.q: Which emblem appears on Parashurama's card in keeping with his identity as the axe-wielding warrior-sage?
questions.explanation: His card bears a bladed-weapon emblem, fitting the sage who wields the axe.

questions.q: In which major epic does Parashurama famously confront the prince Rama after the breaking of Shiva's bow?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Parashurama appears after Rama breaks Shiva's bow at Sita's svayamvara and challenges him.

questions.q: Parashurama is said to have served as a martial guru to which warrior of the Mahabharata, teaching him the use of arms?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata relates that Karna learned weaponry from Parashurama, having approached him as a brahmin; the sage later cursed him on discovering he was not a brahmin.

questions.q: Besides Karna, which grandsire of the Kuru and Pandava princes is described in the Mahabharata as a pupil of Parashurama in archery?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata names Bhishma among the disciples of Parashurama, and the two later fought a great duel.

questions.q: Parashurama belongs to the lineage of which celebrated sage, the founder of the Bhargava line?
questions.explanation: Through Jamadagni, Parashurama is a Bhargava, descended from the sage Bhrigu.

questions.q: Coastal traditions of western India credit Parashurama with reclaiming new land in what manner?
options: Hurling his axe to make the sea recede
questions.explanation: Regional legends say Parashurama cast his axe into the sea, and the land that emerged became the western coastal region.

questions.q: How many times over does tradition say Parashurama purged the earth of the offending kshatriya warriors?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Parashurama cleared the earth of the corrupt kshatriyas twenty-one times.

questions.q: At which sacred field does the Mahabharata say Parashurama offered oblations to his ancestors after his campaign against the kshatriyas?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata associates Parashurama's offerings to the Pitris with Samanta-panchaka, the holy field near Kurukshetra.

questions.q: After his campaigns, to whom is Parashurama said in tradition to have given away the conquered earth in a great sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Parashurama bestowed the entire earth upon the sage Kashyapa as a sacrificial gift.

questions.q: By what means was Renuka restored after Parashurama beheaded her, in the well-known Puranic account?
questions.explanation: Pleased with his obedience, Jamadagni offered Parashurama a boon, which he used to have his mother Renuka brought back to life.

questions.q: Which wish-fulfilling cow in Jamadagni's hermitage did Kartavirya Arjuna covet, setting in motion the feud with Parashurama's family?
questions.explanation: Kartavirya Arjuna's attempt to seize the wish-granting cow Kamadhenu (also called Surabhi) from Jamadagni's ashram triggered the conflict; Airavata and Uchchaihshravas are Indra's elephant and horse, not cows.

questions.q: In the Ramayana encounter, after testing Rama, what does Parashurama do that signals his withdrawal from active warfare?
options: Yields his martial power and retires to penance
Casts his bow into the sea
questions.explanation: Recognizing Rama as the divine, Parashurama yields his accumulated martial energy and withdraws to ascetic penance, often placed on Mount Mahendra.

questions.q: On which mountain is Parashurama traditionally said to dwell in lasting penance as a chiranjivi?
questions.explanation: Tradition places the immortal Parashurama in austerity on Mount Mahendra (Mahendragiri).

questions.q: Parashurama is unusual among Vishnu's avatars because, unlike Rama or Krishna, he is said to remain present even into which present age?
questions.explanation: As a chiranjivi he is said to endure across the ages, including the present Kali Yuga, whereas avatars like Rama and Krishna completed their earthly lives.
